channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EPR Acquires Montreal’s La Ronde Amusement Park from Six Flags
item.description: Montreal’s iconic amusement park, La Ronde, has officially changed ownership. The park, previously operated by Six Flags, has been purchased by EPR Properties, a U.S.-based real estate investment trus...

item.title: O’Sullivan defends aggressive tactics during parliamentary testimony
item.description: Private investigator Paul O’Sullivan appeared before Parliament's ad hoc committee to address allegations of sending threatening messages to witnesses, including Cedrick Nkabinde, the police ministry’...

item.title: Government to restore traders’ stalls and resolve issues at Mabibo market
item.description: The Tanzanian government has promised to address the difficulties traders are facing at the Mabibo market in Dar es Salaam. Many traders were prevented from accessing their stalls due to conflicts wit...

item.title: Graduating Student Killed, Five Others Injured in Armed Clashes Between Moro Groups in Cotabato
item.description: COTABATO CITY — A graduating high school student, Nadia Ebad Haron, tragically lost her life after being struck by a stray bullet during crossfire between two heavily armed Moro factions along the bor...

item.title: Costco Canada recalls chocolate mousse due to undeclared nuts, offers full refund
item.description: Costco Canada has issued a recall for its Delici Dubai Style chocolate mousse (Item #1990644) due to the possible presence of cashew and macadamia nuts that were not declared on the product label. The...

item.title: Global shipping re-routes as Middle East tensions rise, Cape sea route gains importance
item.description: In March 2026, armed conflicts in Iran, including attacks by Israel and the US, seriously disrupted global maritime trade. Tehran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz forced shipping companies to rethink...

item.title: Highland Park installs community sirens to alert residents of ICE activity
item.description: In Los Angeles’ Highland Park neighborhood, local activists are installing loud, portable sirens to warn residents of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) operations. These small, fire engin...

item.title: Remembering Ebo Taylor: Ghanaian Highlife Legend and Global Music Ambassador
item.description: Ebo Taylor, born on 7 January 1936 in Cape Coast, Ghana, was a legendary guitarist, composer, arranger, and a key ambassador of Ghanaian highlife music. His passing on 7 February 2026 marked the loss ...

item.title: PTI leaders remain denied meeting with Imran Khan, call on government for reconsideration
item.description: On Thursday, the Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) could not hold a scheduled meeting with its founder, Imran Khan, who is currently in jail. The day before, PTI Secretary General Salman Akram Raja had s...

item.title: Ebonyi governor clarifies delay in payment of N90,000 minimum wage to teachers
item.description: Governor Francis Nwifuru of Ebonyi State don clear why local governments never fit pay the N90,000 minimum wage wey dey due for teachers under the State Universal Basic Education Board (SUBEB). He tal...

item.title: Senator David Mark Congratulates Former President Obasanjo on 89th Birthday
item.description: On Thursday, March 5, 2026, Sen. David Mark, National Chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), felicitated former President Chief Olusegun Obasanjo on his 89th birthday. In a statement issue...

item.title: How to maximise natural light in your home
item.description: Many homes unintentionally lose natural light due to avoidable design choices. Cayley Scrooby from Sovereign Interiors explains that heavy curtains, dark fabrics, large furniture, and certain material...

item.title: Overtourism in Mexico sparks local backlash amid rising living costs
item.description: Tourism in Mexico has reached unprecedented levels, attracting large numbers of foreign visitors drawn by the nation's vibrant culture and relatively low costs. In the state of Oaxaca, renowned for it...

item.title: Government directs provinces to inspect petrol stations to prevent hoarding amid regional oil supply disruptions
item.description: The federal government has instructed all provincial governments to carry out physical inspections and monitoring of retail petrol stations through deputy commissioners to stop hoarding and undue prof...

item.title: US and Israeli Military Actions Against Iran Violate International Law and Challenge the UN
item.description: On February 16, 2026, economist Jeffrey Sachs warned the UN Security Council that the United States was on the verge of violating the United Nations Charter. Days later, the US and Israel launched a m...

item.title: Family of Lapu Lapu Festival Victim Voices Concerns Over Donation Use
item.description: The family of AJ Sico, who was severely injured during the Lapu Lapu Festival in April, has voiced ongoing concerns over the distribution and use of funds raised for victims. Sico suffered a traumatic...

item.title: Uttarakhand CM Pushkar Singh Dhami joins public celebrations of Holi at official residence
item.description: Dehradun witnessed a colourful celebration as Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami marked the occasion of Holi at his official residence on Wednesday. The Holi Milan Samaroh brought together...

item.title: Vladimir TV adaptation praised for Rachel Weisz's standout performance and nuanced exploration of morality
item.description: This Guardian review celebrates the eight-part Netflix adaptation of Julia May Jonas’s 2022 novel Vladimir as exemplary television for mature audiences. Rachel Weisz delivers a masterful, unflinching ...

item.title: Barbara Kyagulanyi's Assault Highlights Love as a Force in Uganda's Fight for Democracy
item.description: In this opinion piece, Robert Kigongo reflects on the brutal raid and assault on Barbara Itungo Kyagulanyi, wife of Ugandan opposition leader Robert Kyagulanyi (Bobi Wine), which occurred on the night...

item.title: West Indies Team Stranded in India Following T20 World Cup Exit Due to Airspace Restrictions
item.description: The West Indies cricket team has been unable to return home after their exit from the T20 World Cup, following a loss to India in the Super Eight stage on March 1, 2026. The team has remained stranded...
